hypothesis: A two-stage optimization that first performs MLIP-driven energy relaxation (e.g., CHGNet) to bring distorted candidates into the nearest chemically plausible energy basin and then applies symmetry-constrained XRD similarity refinement will produce significantly higher StructureMatcher recovery rates—specifically at least a 20 percentage-point improvement over symmetry-constrained XRD-only gradient descent—for lattice distortions up to σl = 0.1 and coordinate noise up to σc = 0.02.
